texto: It's pretty amazing to see something in your head and write it down, go out and shoot it, then see it on the big screen. It's a pretty amazing feeling.

texto: The best thing about doing those Hallmark movies is my dad loves them. My dad watches all of those Christmas movies, not just ones I'm in. He watches them all, so the first one I did, it was like my Christmas present to my dad.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Eric Johnson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “The best thing about doing those Hallmark movies is my dad loves them. My dad watches all of thos…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, inspiracional, movies pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de natal e inspiracao.

texto: I did my first professional play at 11, and there was really nothing else I ever dreamed of doing. I felt so fortunate that I knew at the age of like, 12, like this is all I want to do for the rest of my life; that's the only play. There's no backup plan. My mom wanted me to go to school and have a backup plan. I'm like 'No, this is the only plan.'
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Eric Johnson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “I did my first professional play at 11, and there was really nothing else I ever dreamed of doing…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, vida, age pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de age e school.

texto: Growing up in Canada, none of my family were performers or anything like that, but I was terrible at hockey, so they needed something for me to do on Saturdays for me to get out of the house. I signed up for theater school on Saturdays, and I'd go for four-and-a-half hours every Saturday morning and learn about theater.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Eric Johnson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. frase_original: Growing up in Canada, none of my family were performers or anything like that, but I was terrible at hockey, so they needed something for me to do on Saturdays for me to get out of the house. I signed up for theater school on Saturdays, and I'd go for four-and-a-half hours every Saturday morning and learn about theater.
